blocks.content:
The next morning came softer.

Ethan noticed that immediately.

Not because the light was different.

It wasn’t.

Still pale.

Still stretching slowly over rooftops and quiet streets.

Still the same city waking up in the same predictable way.

But it felt lighter.

Like something had shifted just enough to let something else in.

He tied his shoes slower than usual.

Checked his phone once.

Nothing new.

That was fine.

He wasn’t expecting anything.

He stepped outside.

The air was cool.

Familiar.

Grounding.

But the run didn’t feel the same anymore.

He wasn’t just moving through the city.

He was moving toward something.

That was new.

Ethan turned the corner toward the park without thinking about it.

The fountain came into view again.

Same uneven stream.

Same worn stone.

Same quiet presence.

But this time—

he didn’t stop.

He slowed.

Just enough to glance at it.

Then kept going.

Because today wasn’t about where things started.

It was about where they were going.
